_comment: Benchmark manifest v2 (docs/23, docs/45, docs/48). SAME 10 pins as manifest.v1.json (identical repo+commit+target -> identical code-under-test, so v1's reproducibility/baseline comparison is untouched and v1 stays the frozen pin), ENRICHED with the value-judgment dimension: business-invariant tags (docs/45 S1) + anchoring invariant DESCRIPTORS (docs/48, source=manifest). These are DECLARED INTENT, never verified value: advisory, carried read-only, change no verdict (conclusion stays NEED_HUMAN_REVIEW). Invariants are method-scoped (no target_lines: exact line numbers can't be pinned offline; method scope is enough for docs/48 _scope + docs/49 survivors). Statements are grounded in the pinned target behavior recorded in docs/50_benchmark/23_BENCHMARK_MANIFEST.md. Run like v1 (jacoco.skip; coverage advisory). source=manifest makes each invariant anchoring (may drive real verification); a model-declared invariant would stay non-anchoring (docs/48 S2).

cases.name: commons-cli Option
cases.expected_invariant: addValue always throws UnsupportedOperationException; getValues() returns null (not an empty array) when the option has no values

invariants.statement: Option.addValue(String) always throws UnsupportedOperationException; values are populated by the parser, never set directly
getValues() returns null (not an empty array) when the option has no values -- a documented Javadoc/impl conflict; the test must pin actual behavior, not the doc

cases.name: commons-cli Options
cases.expected_invariant: adding a required option is reflected in getRequiredOptions()

invariants.statement: addOption registers the option; an option added as required appears in getRequiredOptions()
hasOption(opt) is true iff that option was parsed into the CommandLine; getOptionValue returns the parsed value or null

cases.name: commons-csv CSVRecord
cases.expected_invariant: get(String) throws IllegalArgumentException on an unmapped header; get(int) throws on an out-of-range index

invariants.statement: get(String) throws IllegalArgumentException when the name is not mapped to a header (or the mapping is inconsistent)
get(int) throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException for an index outside the record's range

cases.name: commons-csv CSVFormat
cases.expected_invariant: with* methods are immutable -- they return a new CSVFormat with only the changed setting and leave the original unchanged

invariants.statement: withDelimiter (and other with*/builder methods) returns a NEW CSVFormat carrying only the changed setting; the original instance is unchanged (immutability)

cases.name: commons-text WordUtils
cases.expected_invariant: capitalize(null) returns null; otherwise the first letter of each whitespace-delimited word is upper-cased

invariants.statement: capitalize(null) returns null; for non-null input it upper-cases the first letter of each whitespace-delimited word and leaves the rest unchanged

cases.name: commons-text StringEscapeUtils
cases.expected_invariant: escape maps special characters to entities and unescape is its inverse (round-trip)

invariants.statement: escapeXml10 maps <, >, &, ", ' to their XML entities; unescapeXml is the inverse for those entities (round-trip). Exact entity maps must not be guessed beyond evidence

cases.name: commons-lang3 NumberUtils
cases.expected_invariant: toInt/toDouble/toLong return the supplied default for null or unparseable input and never throw

invariants.statement: toInt(String, default) (and toDouble/toLong) returns the supplied default for null or unparseable input and never throws

cases.name: commons-lang3 Validate
cases.expected_invariant: notNull throws NullPointerException on null; isTrue throws IllegalArgumentException on a false expression; both pass silently otherwise

invariants.statement: notNull throws NullPointerException when the argument is null and returns the argument unchanged otherwise
isTrue throws IllegalArgumentException when the expression is false and is a no-op when true

cases.name: commons-lang3 BooleanUtils
cases.expected_invariant: toBoolean(String) maps 'true'/'yes'/'on' (case-insensitive) to true and null/anything else to false

invariants.statement: toBoolean(String) maps 'true'/'yes'/'on' (case-insensitive) to true and null or any other string to false
